---

## Why this is a no-op

The v1 2026-04-24 proposal (same file prefix, earlier today) already analyzed and issued detailed edit proposals for the active drift class:

- Duplicate PRD variants (`EU_Solicit_PRD_v1.md`, `EU_Solicit_PRD_v2.md`, `planning-artifacts/prd.md`, `planning-artifacts/PRD.md` — case-colliding).
- Duplicate architecture locations (hyphen vs underscore directory + legacy v4/v5 briefs).
- Duplicate UX locations (hyphen vs underscore directory + legacy supplement).
- Directory collision: `planning-artifacts/` (config-canonical, hyphen) vs `planning_artifacts/` (anomaly, underscore).

v1 closed with an explicit exit gate (§ "Exit gate for future re-fires"):

> **Re-fire this workflow today ONLY if:**
> (a) a NEW class of drift signal appears, OR
> (b) a retrospective opens, OR
> (c) a critical blocker emerges that is not already in the v4 carry-forward list.
>
> **Do NOT re-fire on:**
> - The same duplicate-document signal if consolidation has landed.
> - A readiness-report `.md` write alone (ACT-V3-01 still pending).
> - Elapsed time.

This re-fire matches the **do-not-re-fire** clauses. Per v1's own exit gate, the correct action is to record the re-fire and abort.

## Current state verification (2026-04-24, re-fire time)

`ls eusolicit-docs/` confirms v1's consolidation actions have **NOT landed**:

| v1 success criterion | Current state | Status |
|----------------------|---------------|--------|
| Single PRD in `planning-artifacts/` | Both `prd.md` and `PRD.md` still present (case collision) | ❌ Not done |
| `planning_artifacts/` (underscore) deleted | Still present; contains `architecture.md`, `PRD.md`, `ux-spec.md` | ❌ Not done |
| `planning-artifacts/archive/` exists with legacy artifacts | Directory does not exist | ❌ Not done |
| Legacy `EU_Solicit_*.md` moved into archive | Still at `eusolicit-docs/` root | ❌ Not done |
| `ux-design-specification.md` archived | Still alongside `ux-spec.md` | ❌ Not done |

**Interpretation:** The v1 proposal was produced, but its handoff (Developer / Tech Writer) has not yet executed. This is a pending-work state, not a new drift.

No NEW class of drift has appeared since v1 (13:06). Sprint-execution state (stories in `review`, E10 kickoff queue, E13 hardening items) is unchanged from v1's carry-forward table. No retrospective has opened. No critical blocker has emerged outside v4's carry-forward list.

## Action — None

Per v1's exit gate, this workflow aborts without producing new edit proposals.

**What remains true from v1:**
- v1's §4 edit proposals (PRD/architecture/UX consolidation, `planning_artifacts/` deletion, archive README) are still the canonical change set.
- v1's §5 handoff (Developer / Tech Writer primary; Orchestrator secondary) is still the active handoff.
- v4's carry-forward actions (ACT-V2-carry, ACT-V3-01..04, ACT-V4-01..03) remain open.

**What this addendum adds:**
- An audit-trail marker that the drift signal re-fired at least once after v1 without the consolidation work having landed.
- A data point for the Orchestrator team that ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) and ACT-V3-02 (wire drift threshold) are now **empirically causing redundant course-correction re-fires**. This strengthens the case for prioritizing those two actions.

---

## Recommendation to Orchestrator team (no escalation, just signal)

- **Priority bump for ACT-V3-01:** the "exclude readiness-report `.md` writes from drift triggers" action has now measurably produced ≥1 redundant course-correction run after v1 landed. Promoting from P1 to P0 would stop same-day re-fires.
- **Priority bump for ACT-V3-02:** wiring the drift threshold (backlog<10 / epic-transition / retro-open) would route the brief "Drift detected. Review requirements." to a threshold check before firing the full workflow.

Neither item is scope for this workflow run; flagging only.
